entries.title: Mean (Arithmetic Average)
entries.content: Sum of all data values divided by the count. Sensitive to outliers. For grouped data, use midpoint × frequency.

entries.title: Standard Deviation
entries.content: Measures spread of data around the mean. Population uses N; sample uses n-1 (Bessel correction) for unbiased estimate.

entries.title: Probability of Independent Events
entries.content: If events A and B are independent, the probability both occur is the product of individual probabilities.

entries.title: Bayes' Theorem
entries.content: Updates probability of a hypothesis given new evidence. Combines prior belief with likelihood of observed data.

entries.title: Normal Distribution (68-95-99.7 Rule)
entries.content: For a normal distribution, approximately 68% of data falls within 1σ of mean, 95% within 2σ, and 99.7% within 3σ.
